title: PDF Invoice Generator

summary.overview: This PR adds a PDF invoice generation capability to the billing service, supporting both single and batch invoice creation. The implementation leverages a well-structured template system and follows existing service patterns closely. Code quality is excellent with thorough test coverage and clean separation of concerns.

riskAssessment.summary: The change is well-contained within the billing domain with no modifications to existing business logic. The PDF generation library is a mature, widely-used dependency with no known vulnerabilities.
items: New dependency on pdfkit library adds ~2MB to the service bundle size
Large batch invoice generation could increase memory usage temporarily

strengths: Template system cleanly separates presentation from business logic, making it easy to update invoice layouts without touching service code
Batch generation uses streaming to keep memory usage constant regardless of invoice count
Comprehensive test suite covers edge cases including multi-currency formatting, tax calculation rounding, and empty line items
Follows the existing controller-service-repository pattern established in the billing service codebase
Proper error handling with domain-specific exceptions that map to appropriate HTTP status codes
Invoice number generation is thread-safe and guarantees uniqueness using the existing sequence generator

issues.message: The company logo path is hardcoded as a relative file path which may not resolve correctly in containerized deployments where the working directory differs.
issues.recommendation: Use path.resolve with __dirname or load the logo from the existing asset configuration to ensure consistent resolution across all deployment environments.

recommendations: Add a configurable file size limit for batch PDF generation to prevent potential memory issues with extremely large batches in production
Consider adding PDF/A compliance for invoices that may need long-term archival per regulatory requirements
